comment: Curated prices for models the upstream LiteLLM catalog does not price. Merged into the generated bundled catalog by tools/genprices; never overwritten by regeneration. Entries stay in the 'bundled' layer on purpose: a fresher 'litellm' catalog outranks bundled on catalog_effective, so if upstream ever starts pricing a model here, upstream wins automatically and the curated row stops mattering. Promoting an entry to 'official' would outrank upstream forever and freeze a stale price.
curation_bar: A 'vendor_rate' entry needs a real vendor rate-card source_url, a confirmed rate, and a named human verifier with the date they checked. An 'equivalent_estimate' entry is the single narrower exception, for a real released subscription-only model the vendor prices nowhere: it must name a vendor-priced basis_model, carry that basis model's exact rate (enforced against the upstream catalog at generation time), and state in pricing_note that the value is an equivalent token cost rather than an actual subscription invoice; verified_by/verified_on then name who approved the estimate and its basis. A model that is absent, unreleased, or cannot be identified confidently stays unpriced and belongs in 'pending' below. UNPRICED is a correct answer; a silent guess is not.

entries.pricing_note: Equivalent token-cost estimate, not an OpenAI price for this model and not what a Pro subscription actually bills. Spark is a released Pro-subscription-only successor to gpt-5.3-codex that OpenAI exposes through no API and prices on no rate card, so this row carries the vendor rate of its predecessor gpt-5.3-codex to answer 'what would these tokens have cost' instead of leaving the usage unpriced. It disappears the moment upstream publishes a real rate.
